memoryArtifacts.description:
Birth: 	Jun. 18, 1858
Decatur
Otsego County
New York, USA
Death: 	Jan. 5, 1939
White Salmon
Klickitat County
Washington, USA

Father: Slyvanus Covey
Mother: Sarah (Fowler) Covey

Wife: Carrie A. Covey
Son: Ernest Covey
Daughters: Ruth White and 
Leona Breedlove

He was 80 years old at the time of his death.
Information from Washington State Digital Archives website.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Silvanus Hartwell Covey於1858年6月18日出生在美國紐約州奧瑟格德卡度時，他的父親Sylvanis S Covey Sr40歲，母親Sarah Fowler35歲。他於1886年與Carrie A Utter結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1880年住在美國紐約州布盧桑福德。他去世於1939年1月5日在美國華盛頓州可利吉塔特懷特 賽門享年80歲，埋葬在美國華盛頓州可利吉塔特懷特 賽門。

nameMeanings.name: Covey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish: shortened form of MacCovey, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Cobhthaigh (see Coffey ).

English: unexplained. Covey is used of ‘pantry’ in the early modern period and covey ‘little chap’ (a diminutive of cove, Middle English cof ‘bold, eager, courageous’; see Cove ) in the early nineteenth century, but there is no evidence to confirm either as the source for the surname.

variants.name: Coffey
meanings: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Cathbhadha ‘descendant of Cathbhadh’, a byname meaning ‘battle tent’, Ó Cathbhuadhaigh ‘descendant of Cathbhuadhach’ (‘battle victorious’), or Ó Cathmhogha ‘descendant of Cathmhugh’ (‘battle slave’).
shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Cobhthaigh ‘descendant of Cobhthach’, a byname meaning ‘victorious’.

nameMeanings.name: Silvanus
meanings: From the Roman name Silvanus or Sylvanus; also the appellation of a god of forests and fields; from silva, sylva, a forest, wood. Hence by corruption the name Silas.

nameMeanings.name: Hartwell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (West Midlands): habitational name from any of several places called Hartwell (Staffordshire, Northamptonshire, Buckinghamshire, Sussex, and Devon), all named with Old English heorot ‘hart, stag’ + wella ‘well, spring, stream’.

historicEvents.description: Abraham Lincoln issues Emancipation Proclamation, declaring slaves in Confederate states to be free.

historicEvents.title: The Battle at Gettysburg
historicEvents.description: The Battle of Gettysburg involved the largest number of casualties of the entire Civil war and is often described as the war's turning point. Between 46,000 and 51,000 soldiers lost their lives during the three-day Battle. To honor the fallen soldiers, President Abraham Lincoln read his historic Gettysburg Address and helped those listening by redefining the purpose of the war.

historicEvents.title: The Assassination of James Garfield
historicEvents.description: Garfield was shot twice by Charles J. Guitea at Railroad Station in Washington, D.C. on July 2, 1881. After eleven weeks of intensive and other care Garfield died in Elberon, New Jersey, the second of four presidents to be assassinated, following Abraham Lincoln.

historicEvents.title: Washington Becomes a State
historicEvents.description: On November 11, 1889, Washington Territory became Washington State the 42nd state to enter the Union. The state was named in honor of George Washington.

historicEvents.title: Assassination of Mckinley
historicEvents.description: President William McKinley was shot at the Temple of Music, in the Pan-American Exposition, while shaking hands with the public. Leon Czolgosz shot him twice in the abdomen because he thought it was his duty to do so. McKinley died after eight days of watch and care. He was the third American president to be assassinated. After his death, Congress passed legislation to officially make the Secret Service and gave them responsibility for protecting the President at all times.

historicEvents.title: Pike's Market
historicEvents.description: Pike’s Market is one of the oldest still working farmer’s markets in the US. It is located in Seattle’s central business district, just north of Belltown , and southwest of central waterfront and Elliott Bay. One of the attractions there is the gum wall.

historicEvents.title: The President Dies of a Heart Attack
historicEvents.description: Warrant G. Harding died of a heart attack in the Palace hotel in San Francisco.
historicEvents.urlTitle: The unexpected death of President Harding

historicEvents.title: Amendment of Equal Rights
historicEvents.description: Is a proposed amendment to help guarantee equal legal rights for all citizens of the United States. Its main objective is to end legal distinctions between the two genders in terms of divorce, property, employment, and other legal matters. Even though it isn't the 28th Amendment yet, it has started conversations about the meaning of legal equality.

en.lifeSummary: When Silvanus Hartwell Covey was born on 18 June 1858, in Decatur, Otsego, New York, United States, his father, Sylvanis S Covey Sr, was 40 and his mother, Sarah Fowler, was 35. He married Carrie A Utter in 1886. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He lived in Sanford, Broome, New York, United States in 1880. He died on 5 January 1939, in White Salmon, Klickitat, Washington, United States, at the age of 80, and was buried in White Salmon, Klickitat, Washington, United States.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Silvanus Hartwell Covey nació el 18 de junio de 1858, en Decatur, Otsego, Nueva York, Estados Unidos, su papá, Sylvanis S Covey Sr, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Sarah Fowler, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Carrie A Utter en 1886. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Vivió en Sanford, Broome, Nueva York, Estados Unidos en 1880. Murió el 5 de enero de 1939, en White Salmon, Klickitat, Washington, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 80 años, y fue sepultado en White Salmon, Klickitat, Washington, Estados Unidos.
